title_en: The Forever Home Reveals a Terrible Future Where Only One Ultra-Wealthy Family Is Safe From the Climate Apocalypse
summary_en: What happens when the ultimate refuge from the climate apocalypse is finally breached? Find out in this stunning preview of Oni Press' The Forever Home.
body_en:
Writer/artist Christian Ward is on a hot streak lately, with the Event Horizon prequel Dark Descent proving to be a huge critical and commercial success for IDW Publishing. This time, Ward is teaming up with Abbott artist Sami Kivelä for The Forever Home. What happens when an ultra-wealthy family creates the perfect refuge from the climate apocalypse, only for that refuge to be breached? We can't wait to find out.
IGN can exclusively debut the first preview art for The Forever Home #1. Get a closer look in the slideshow gallery below:
Here's the official summary for The Forever Home:
“The horror of The Forever Home was easy to find,” said Ward in a statement. “It's in the ever-increasing news stories that seem to surround us. Stories of a changing world. Of more and more extreme weather. Where it's not hard to imagine people in the world whose survival is being pushed to the brink by that change and of course, it's equally not hard to believe the conspiracies of the billionaires building themselves bunkers and liferafts for imagined apocalypse. That's the angry seed from which we grow the story of this book. Soaked in the terror that sometimes you have to go impossible lengths to keep your family safe.”
“The Forever Home is like the perfect Ray Bradbury short story by way of Parasite,” said Oni Press Editor-in Chief Sierra Hahn. “Christian and Sami’s vision is haunting, prescient, and an unforgettable look into the future of the world, with cliffhangers worthy of Brian K Vaughan. It's exceptional storytelling.”
The Forever Home #1 will be released on September 9. You can preorder a copy at your local comic shop .
For more, learn about The Muppets Take the Marvel Universe and see an exclusive preview of Vampire Survivors #1 .
Jesse is a mild-mannered staff writer for IGN. Allow him to lend a machete to your intellectual thicket by following @jschedeen on BlueSky .
